docs.title: Dokime
docs.text:
The open-source workbench for ML training data.

Dokime lets you filter, deduplicate, embed, search, and score your training data -- from one pip install.

pip install dokime-ai

docs.title: What can it do?
docs.text:
from dokime.core.pipeline import Pipeline
from dokime.core.filters import LengthFilter, WhitespaceFilter
from dokime.quality.dedup import ExactDedup

pipeline = Pipeline("my-curation")
pipeline.add_filter(LengthFilter(min_length=50))
pipeline.add_filter(WhitespaceFilter(max_whitespace_ratio=0.4))
pipeline.add_filter(ExactDedup())

result = pipeline.run("data/raw.jsonl", "data/clean.parquet")

Or from the CLI:

dokime curate data/raw.jsonl data/clean.parquet --min-length 50 --dedup

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Installation -- all extras explained
  • Quick Start -- end-to-end tutorial
  • Filters -- all 12+ built-in filters
  • Pipelines -- Python API and YAML config
  • Embeddings -- search, outliers, semantic dedup
  • Attribution -- find which training examples help or hurt
  • CLI Reference -- all 8 commands

docs.title: dokime.attribution.engine
docs.text:
Attribution engine — wraps TRAK to provide a simple API for data influence scoring.

This is the core of Dokime's differentiator: "which training examples help or hurt your model?"

docs.title: AttributionEngine
docs.text:
Compute data attribution scores for fine-tuning datasets using TRAK.

Answers the question: "Which training examples improve or hurt model performance on a given evaluation set?"

Example::

engine = AttributionEngine(
    model_name="gpt2",
    train_data="finetune_data.jsonl",
    eval_data="eval_data.jsonl",
)
scores = engine.compute()
# scores.shape = (n_eval, n_train)
# Positive = training example helps on this eval example
# Negative = training example hurts on this eval example

harmful = engine.find_harmful(top_n=100)
helpful = engine.find_helpful(top_n=100)

docs.title: aggregate_scores()
docs.text:
Aggregate per-eval scores into a single per-training-example score.

Returns:

Type Description ndarray

Array of shape (n_train,) — average influence of each training example

ndarray

across all eval examples. Higher = more helpful. Lower = more harmful.

docs.title: compute(quiet=False)
docs.text:
Compute attribution scores.

Returns:

Type Description ndarray

Array of shape (n_eval, n_train) with per-example influence scores.

ndarray

Positive values indicate the training example helps on that eval example.

ndarray

Negative values indicate it hurts.

docs.title: find_harmful(top_n=50)
docs.text:
Find training examples that hurt model performance the most.

Returns:

Type Description list[tuple[int, float]]

List of (train_index, average_score) tuples, sorted most harmful first.

docs.title: find_helpful(top_n=50)
docs.text:
Find training examples that help model performance the most.

Returns:

Type Description list[tuple[int, float]]

List of (train_index, average_score) tuples, sorted most helpful first.

docs.title: print_summary()
docs.text: Print a formatted summary of attribution results.

docs.title: summary()
docs.text:
Get a summary of attribution results.

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dict with counts of helpful/harmful examples and score statistics.

docs.title: dokime.core.filters
docs.text: Base filter classes and built-in heuristic filters.

docs.title: AlphaFilter dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Filter

Filter documents with too few alphabetic characters (catches garbage/numeric spam).

docs.title: FieldExistsFilter dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Filter

Filter documents that are missing a required field.

docs.title: Filter
docs.text:
Bases: ABC

Base class for all filters in a curation pipeline.

docs.title: filter(sample) abstractmethod
docs.text: Return True to keep the sample, False to discard it.

docs.title: name() abstractmethod
docs.text: Human-readable name for this filter.

docs.title: LanguageFilter dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Filter

Filter documents by detected language.

Uses lingua-language-detector (pure Python, no compilation needed). Falls back to fastText if installed and lingua is not available.

Requires: pip install dokime-ai[nlp]

Language codes: ISO 639-1 lowercase (e.g., "en", "de", "fr", "zh", "ja").

docs.title: LengthFilter dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Filter

Filter documents by text length (character count).

docs.title: LineCountFilter dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Filter

Filter documents by number of lines.

docs.title: RegexFilter dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Filter

Filter documents matching (or not matching) a regex pattern.

docs.title: RepetitionFilter dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Filter

Filter documents with excessive n-gram repetition.

docs.title: SpecialCharFilter dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Filter

Filter documents with excessive special characters.

docs.title: StopwordFilter dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Filter

Filter documents with too few stopwords (catches keyword spam, lists, code).

docs.title: URLFilter dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Filter

Filter documents with excessive URLs.

docs.title: WhitespaceFilter dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Filter

Filter documents with excessive whitespace ratio.

docs.title: WordCountFilter dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: Filter

Filter documents by word count.

docs.title: dokime.core.pipeline
docs.text: Pipeline orchestration for data curation workflows.

docs.title: Pipeline
docs.text:
A composable data curation pipeline with streaming execution.

Processes documents one at a time through the filter chain, never loading the full dataset into memory. Supports writing output in configurable batch sizes for constant memory usage regardless of dataset size.

Example::

pipeline = Pipeline("my-pipeline")
pipeline.add_filter(LengthFilter(min_length=100))
pipeline.add_filter(WhitespaceFilter(max_whitespace_ratio=0.4))
pipeline.add_filter(MinHashDedup(threshold=0.8))
result = pipeline.run("data/raw.jsonl", "data/curated.parquet")

docs.title: add_filter(f)
docs.text: Add a filter to the pipeline. Returns self for chaining.

docs.title: from_config(config_path) classmethod
docs.text:
Load a pipeline from a YAML config file.

Example YAML::

name: my-pipeline
output_format: parquet          # parquet or jsonl (default: inferred from path)
write_batch_size: 10000         # flush to disk every N docs (default: 10000)

filters:
  - LengthFilter:
      min_length: 100
      max_length: 100000
  - WhitespaceFilter:
      max_whitespace_ratio: 0.4
  - RepetitionFilter:
      max_repetition_ratio: 0.3
  - ExactDedup: {}

docs.title: process(data)
docs.text:
Stream documents through the filter chain, yielding kept documents.

This is the core streaming API — never buffers the full dataset.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default data Iterator[dict[str, Any]] | str

Iterator of document dicts, or a file path string.

required

Yields:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Documents that pass all filters.

docs.title: run(input_path, output_path=None, quiet=False, write_batch_size=10000)
docs.text:
Execute the pipeline with streaming I/O.

Writes output in batches for constant memory usage. Never loads the full dataset into memory.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default input_path str

Path to input dataset (JSONL, Parquet, CSV, or HuggingFace dataset ID).

required output_path str | None

Path for curated output. Format inferred from extension.

None quiet bool

Suppress progress output.

False write_batch_size int

Flush to disk every N kept documents. Lower = less memory.

10000

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Dictionary with pipeline execution statistics.

docs.title: dokime.core.registry
docs.text: Filter registry for YAML config-based pipeline construction.

docs.title: register_filter(name, filter_cls)
docs.text: Register a custom filter class for use in YAML configs.

docs.title: dokime.embeddings.compute
docs.text: Compute embeddings for text documents using sentence-transformers or custom models.

docs.title: EmbeddingModel
docs.text:
Wrapper around sentence-transformers for computing text embeddings.

Example::

model = EmbeddingModel("all-MiniLM-L6-v2")
embeddings = model.encode_texts(["hello world", "foo bar"])

docs.title: encode_texts(texts, batch_size=64, show_progress=True)
docs.text:
Encode a list of texts into embeddings.

Returns:

Type Description ndarray

numpy array of shape (len(texts), embedding_dim)

docs.title: compute_embeddings(data, model_name='all-MiniLM-L6-v2', text_field='text', batch_size=64, output_path=None, device=None, quiet=False)
docs.text:
Compute embeddings for a dataset.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default data Iterator[dict[str, Any]] | list[dict[str, Any]]

Iterator or list of document dicts.

required model_name str

Name of the sentence-transformer model.

'all-MiniLM-L6-v2' text_field str

Field containing the text to embed.

'text' batch_size int

Batch size for encoding.

64 output_path str | Path | None

Optional path to save embeddings as .npy file.

None device str | None

Device for inference ('cpu', 'cuda', etc.).

None quiet bool

Suppress progress output.

False

Returns:

Type Description tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], ndarray]

Tuple of (documents list, embeddings array of shape [n_docs, embed_dim]).

docs.title: dokime.embeddings.search
docs.text: Semantic search and nearest-neighbor operations on embeddings.

docs.title: AnomalyScorer
docs.text:
Score documents by how anomalous they are, using k-NN distance in embedding space.

Low k-NN distance = typical sample (surrounded by similar documents). High k-NN distance = anomalous/outlier (isolated in embedding space).

Example::

scorer = AnomalyScorer(embeddings)
scores = scorer.score_all(k=10)
# scores[i] = average distance to 10 nearest neighbors for document i
outlier_indices = np.argsort(scores)[-100:]  # top 100 outliers

docs.title: find_outliers(k=10, threshold=None, top_n=None)
docs.text:
Find outlier document indices.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default k int

Number of neighbors for scoring.

10 threshold float | None

Anomaly score threshold. Documents above this are outliers.

None top_n int | None

Return the top N most anomalous documents.

None

Returns:

Type Description list[int]

List of document indices identified as outliers.

docs.title: score_all(k=10)
docs.text:
Compute anomaly scores for all documents.

Returns:

Type Description ndarray

Array of shape (n_docs,) with anomaly scores.

ndarray

Higher score = more anomalous (lower average similarity to neighbors).

docs.title: EmbeddingIndex
docs.text:
FAISS-based index for fast nearest-neighbor search over embeddings.

Example::

index = EmbeddingIndex(embeddings, documents)
results = index.search("machine learning", model, k=5)
for r in results:
    print(f"{r.score:.3f}: {r.document['text'][:80]}")

docs.title: find_neighbors(idx, k=10)
docs.text: Find the k nearest neighbors of a document by its index.

docs.title: search(query, model, k=10)
docs.text:
Search for documents similar to a text query.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default query str

Text query to search for.

required model Any

EmbeddingModel instance for encoding the query.

required k int

Number of results to return.

10

Returns:

Type Description list[SearchResult]

List of SearchResult objects, sorted by similarity (highest first).

docs.title: search_by_vector(vector, k=10)
docs.text: Search for documents similar to a given embedding vector.

docs.title: SearchResult dataclass
docs.text: A single search result with document, score, and index.

docs.title: dokime.embeddings.dedup
docs.text: Semantic deduplication using embeddings — finds near-duplicates that hash-based methods miss.

docs.title: deduplicate_by_embeddings(embeddings, documents, threshold=0.95, quiet=False)
docs.text:
Remove semantic duplicates, keeping the first occurrence.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default embeddings ndarray

Array of shape (n_docs, embed_dim), L2-normalized.

required documents list[dict[str, Any]]

List of document dicts.

required threshold float

Cosine similarity threshold.

0.95 quiet bool

Suppress output.

False

Returns:

Type Description tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[int]]

Tuple of (deduplicated documents, indices of kept documents).

docs.title: find_semantic_duplicates(embeddings, documents, threshold=0.95, quiet=False)
docs.text:
Find pairs of semantically similar documents using embedding cosine similarity.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default embeddings ndarray

Array of shape (n_docs, embed_dim), L2-normalized.

required documents list[dict[str, Any]]

List of document dicts (for reference).

required threshold float

Cosine similarity threshold to consider as duplicate (0-1).

0.95 quiet bool

Suppress output.

False

Returns:

Type Description list[tuple[int, int, float]]

List of (idx_a, idx_b, similarity) tuples for duplicate pairs.

docs.title: Base install
docs.text:
pip install dokime-ai

This gives you heuristic filters, exact deduplication, the CLI, and JSONL/Parquet/CSV I/O. No heavy dependencies.

docs.title: Extras
docs.text:
Install only what you need:

pip install "dokime-ai[embeddings]"   # sentence-transformers, FAISS search, semantic dedup
pip install "dokime-ai[dedup]"        # MinHash-LSH fuzzy deduplication
pip install "dokime-ai[nlp]"          # language detection (lingua)
pip install "dokime-ai[io]"           # HuggingFace datasets, Pandas
pip install "dokime-ai[attribution]"  # TRAK-based data attribution (torch, transformers)
pip install "dokime-ai[explore]"      # web UI (FastAPI)

Or everything at once:

pip install "dokime-ai[all]"

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/dokime-ai/dokime.git
cd dokime
pip install -e ".[dev]"
pre-commit install

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • No GPU required (but recommended for embeddings and attribution)

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This tutorial walks through the full Dokime workflow: filter, deduplicate, embed, search, and detect outliers.

docs.title: 1. Build a pipeline
docs.text:
from dokime.core.pipeline import Pipeline
from dokime.core.filters import LengthFilter, WhitespaceFilter, RepetitionFilter
from dokime.quality.dedup import ExactDedup

pipeline = Pipeline("my-curation")
pipeline.add_filter(LengthFilter(min_length=50, max_length=100_000))
pipeline.add_filter(WhitespaceFilter(max_whitespace_ratio=0.4))
pipeline.add_filter(RepetitionFilter(max_repetition_ratio=0.3))
pipeline.add_filter(ExactDedup())

result = pipeline.run("data/raw.jsonl", "data/curated.jsonl")
print(f"Kept {result['total_kept']} / {result['total_read']} documents")

docs.title: 2. Same thing from the CLI
docs.text:
dokime curate data/raw.jsonl data/curated.jsonl \
  --min-length 50 --max-whitespace 0.4 --max-repetition 0.3 --dedup

docs.title: 3. Or with a YAML config
docs.text:
# pipeline.yaml
name: my-curation
filters:
  - LengthFilter:
      min_length: 50
      max_length: 100000
  - WhitespaceFilter:
      max_whitespace_ratio: 0.4
  - RepetitionFilter:
      max_repetition_ratio: 0.3
  - ExactDedup: {}

dokime curate data/raw.jsonl data/curated.jsonl --config pipeline.yaml

docs.title: 4. Compute embeddings and search
docs.text:
from dokime.embeddings.compute import EmbeddingModel, compute_embeddings
from dokime.embeddings.search import EmbeddingIndex
from dokime.io.readers import auto_read

docs, embeddings = compute_embeddings(
    data=auto_read("data/curated.jsonl"),
    model_name="all-MiniLM-L6-v2",
)

model = EmbeddingModel("all-MiniLM-L6-v2")
index = EmbeddingIndex(embeddings, docs)
results = index.search("how to remove duplicates", model, k=5)
for r in results:
    print(f"[{r.score:.3f}] {r.document['text'][:80]}")

docs.title: 5. Find outliers
docs.text:
from dokime.embeddings.search import AnomalyScorer

scorer = AnomalyScorer(embeddings)
outlier_indices = scorer.find_outliers(k=10, top_n=5)

See the full examples directory for runnable scripts.

docs.title: Data Attribution
docs.text:
Dokime's attribution engine answers: "Which training examples help or hurt your model?"

It uses TRAK to compute per-example influence scores. Requires dokime-ai[attribution] (installs torch, transformers, traker).

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from dokime.attribution.engine import AttributionEngine

engine = AttributionEngine(
    model_name="gpt2",
    train_data="train.jsonl",
    eval_data="eval.jsonl",
)

scores = engine.compute()
# scores.shape = (n_eval, n_train)
# Positive = training example helps eval performance
# Negative = training example hurts eval performance

harmful = engine.find_harmful(top_n=50)   # [(train_idx, score), ...]
helpful = engine.find_helpful(top_n=50)
engine.print_summary()

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
dokime attribute train.jsonl eval.jsonl \
  --model gpt2 \
  --top 20 \
  --save-dir ./attribution_results

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. A proxy model (default: GPT-2) is loaded
  2. Training examples are featurized using TRAK's random projection
  3. Each eval example is scored against all training examples
  4. Positive scores mean the training example improves performance on that eval example; negative scores mean it hurts

docs.title: When to use this
docs.text:
• Cleaning fine-tuning data: remove harmful examples before training
  • Debugging model failures: trace bad outputs back to training data
  • Data valuation: understand which examples matter most

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Default Description model_name gpt2 HuggingFace model for proxy max_length 512 Max token length proj_dim 2048 TRAK projection dimension device auto cpu or cuda save_dir temp dir Where to save TRAK artifacts

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: All commands are available via the dokime command after installation.

docs.title: dokime version
docs.text:
Print the installed version.

dokime version

docs.title: dokime curate
docs.text:
Run a curation pipeline on a dataset.

# With flags
dokime curate input.jsonl output.parquet \
  --min-length 50 --max-length 100000 \
  --max-whitespace 0.4 --max-repetition 0.3 --max-special 0.3 \
  --dedup --fuzzy-dedup 0.8

# With YAML config
dokime curate input.jsonl output.parquet --config pipeline.yaml
Flag Description --config, -c Path to YAML pipeline config --min-length Minimum document length (chars) --max-length Maximum document length (chars) --max-whitespace Max whitespace ratio (0-1) --max-repetition Max n-gram repetition ratio (0-1) --max-special Max special character ratio (0-1) --dedup Enable exact deduplication --fuzzy-dedup MinHash dedup threshold (0-1)

docs.title: dokime stats
docs.text:
Show basic statistics about a dataset.

dokime stats data/raw.jsonl

Outputs document count, total characters, average/min/max length.

docs.title: dokime embed
docs.text:
Compute embeddings and save as .npy. Requires dokime-ai[embeddings].

dokime embed data/curated.jsonl embeddings.npy \
  --model all-MiniLM-L6-v2 --batch-size 64 --device cuda

docs.title: dokime search
docs.text:
Semantic search over a dataset. Requires dokime-ai[embeddings].

dokime search data/curated.jsonl "your query here" -k 10

# With precomputed embeddings (faster)
dokime search data/curated.jsonl "your query" -e embeddings.npy

docs.title: dokime outliers
docs.text:
Find anomalous documents via k-NN distance. Requires dokime-ai[embeddings].

dokime outliers data/curated.jsonl --top 20 -k 10

# With precomputed embeddings
dokime outliers data/curated.jsonl -e embeddings.npy --top 20

docs.title: dokime attribute
docs.text:
Score training data influence on model performance. Requires dokime-ai[attribution].

dokime attribute train.jsonl eval.jsonl \
  --model gpt2 --top 20 --save-dir ./results

docs.title: dokime explore
docs.text:
Launch an interactive web UI. Requires dokime-ai[explore].

dokime explore data/curated.jsonl --port 8765 -e embeddings.npy

docs.title: Embeddings
docs.text: Dokime uses sentence-transformers to compute embeddings and FAISS for fast similarity search. Requires dokime-ai[embeddings].

docs.title: Compute embeddings
docs.text:
from dokime.embeddings.compute import compute_embeddings
from dokime.io.readers import auto_read

docs, embeddings = compute_embeddings(
    data=auto_read("data/curated.jsonl"),
    model_name="all-MiniLM-L6-v2",
    batch_size=64,
)
# embeddings.shape = (n_docs, 384)

Save to disk:

dokime embed data/curated.jsonl embeddings.npy --model all-MiniLM-L6-v2

docs.title: Semantic search
docs.text:
Find documents similar to a natural language query:

from dokime.embeddings.compute import EmbeddingModel
from dokime.embeddings.search import EmbeddingIndex

model = EmbeddingModel("all-MiniLM-L6-v2")
index = EmbeddingIndex(embeddings, docs)
results = index.search("data deduplication methods", model, k=5)

for r in results:
    print(f"[{r.score:.3f}] {r.document['text'][:80]}")

Or from the CLI:

dokime search data/curated.jsonl "data deduplication methods" -k 5

docs.title: Outlier detection
docs.text:
Surface anomalous documents using k-NN distance scoring:

from dokime.embeddings.search import AnomalyScorer

scorer = AnomalyScorer(embeddings)
outlier_indices = scorer.find_outliers(k=10, top_n=20)
scores = scorer.score_all(k=10)

dokime outliers data/curated.jsonl --top 20

docs.title: Semantic deduplication
docs.text:
Find near-duplicate documents by embedding cosine similarity:

from dokime.embeddings.dedup import find_semantic_duplicates

pairs = find_semantic_duplicates(embeddings, docs, threshold=0.90)
for idx_a, idx_b, similarity in pairs:
    print(f"Pair ({idx_a}, {idx_b}): {similarity:.3f}")

docs.title: Filters
docs.text: All filters inherit from dokime.core.filters.Filter. They return True to keep a sample, False to discard it. Every filter accepts a text_field parameter (default: "text").

docs.title: LengthFilter
docs.text:
Filter by character count.

from dokime.core.filters import LengthFilter
f = LengthFilter(min_length=50, max_length=100_000)

docs.title: WordCountFilter
docs.text:
Filter by word count.

from dokime.core.filters import WordCountFilter
f = WordCountFilter(min_words=10, max_words=50_000)

docs.title: LineCountFilter
docs.text:
Filter by number of lines.

from dokime.core.filters import LineCountFilter
f = LineCountFilter(min_lines=1, max_lines=10_000)

docs.title: WhitespaceFilter
docs.text:
Remove documents with excessive whitespace (formatting junk, empty pages).

from dokime.core.filters import WhitespaceFilter
f = WhitespaceFilter(max_whitespace_ratio=0.4)

docs.title: RepetitionFilter
docs.text:
Catch boilerplate and spam via n-gram repetition ratio.

from dokime.core.filters import RepetitionFilter
f = RepetitionFilter(max_repetition_ratio=0.3, ngram_size=5)

docs.title: SpecialCharFilter
docs.text:
Remove documents overloaded with special characters (encoding artifacts, garbled text).

from dokime.core.filters import SpecialCharFilter
f = SpecialCharFilter(max_special_ratio=0.3)

docs.title: AlphaFilter
docs.text:
Catch numeric spam, base64 blobs, and other non-text content.

from dokime.core.filters import AlphaFilter
f = AlphaFilter(min_alpha_ratio=0.5)

docs.title: URLFilter
docs.text:
Remove URL-heavy documents (link farms, crawl artifacts).

from dokime.core.filters import URLFilter
f = URLFilter(max_url_ratio=0.1)

docs.title: StopwordFilter
docs.text:
Catch keyword spam, code, and list-only content by requiring a minimum stopword presence.

from dokime.core.filters import StopwordFilter
f = StopwordFilter(min_stopword_ratio=0.05)

docs.title: LanguageFilter
docs.text:
Filter by detected language using lingua (or fastText fallback). Requires dokime-ai[nlp].

from dokime.core.filters import LanguageFilter
f = LanguageFilter(languages=["en", "de"], min_confidence=0.5)

docs.title: FieldExistsFilter
docs.text:
Ensure a required field is present and non-empty.

from dokime.core.filters import FieldExistsFilter
f = FieldExistsFilter(required_field="text")

docs.title: RegexFilter
docs.text:
Include or exclude documents matching a regex pattern.

from dokime.core.filters import RegexFilter

# Exclude boilerplate
f = RegexFilter(pattern=r"(?i)cookie policy|terms of service", exclude=True)

# Keep only documents containing a keyword
f = RegexFilter(pattern=r"machine learning", exclude=False)

docs.title: ExactDedup
docs.text:
SHA-256 exact deduplication. No extra dependencies.

from dokime.quality.dedup import ExactDedup
f = ExactDedup()

docs.title: MinHashDedup
docs.text:
Fuzzy deduplication via MinHash-LSH. Requires dokime-ai[dedup].

from dokime.quality.dedup import MinHashDedup
f = MinHashDedup(threshold=0.8, num_perm=128)

docs.title: Pipelines
docs.text: Pipelines chain filters together and run them over a dataset. You can build them in Python or load from YAML.

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from dokime.core.pipeline import Pipeline
from dokime.core.filters import LengthFilter, WhitespaceFilter
from dokime.quality.dedup import ExactDedup

pipeline = Pipeline("my-pipeline")
pipeline.add_filter(LengthFilter(min_length=50))
pipeline.add_filter(WhitespaceFilter(max_whitespace_ratio=0.4))
pipeline.add_filter(ExactDedup())

result = pipeline.run("input.jsonl", "output.parquet")

run() returns a stats dict with total_read, total_kept, total_removed, removal_rate_pct, per_filter_removed, and docs_per_second.

docs.title: Dry run
docs.text:
Pass output_path=None to run without writing:

stats = pipeline.run("input.jsonl", None)

docs.title: YAML config
docs.text:
name: my-pipeline
filters:
  - LengthFilter:
      min_length: 50
      max_length: 100000
  - WhitespaceFilter:
      max_whitespace_ratio: 0.4
  - ExactDedup: {}

Load and run:

pipeline = Pipeline.from_config("pipeline.yaml")
pipeline.run("input.jsonl", "output.parquet")

Or from the CLI:

dokime curate input.jsonl output.parquet --config pipeline.yaml

docs.title: Supported formats
docs.text:
Format Read Write JSONL Yes Yes Parquet Yes Yes CSV Yes -- HuggingFace datasets Yes (with [io]) --

Output format is inferred from the file extension (.parquet or .jsonl).

docs.title: Custom filters
docs.text:
Any class inheriting from Filter works in a pipeline. Register it for YAML support:

from dokime.core.filters import Filter
from dokime.core.registry import register_filter

class MyFilter(Filter):
    def filter(self, sample):
        return len(sample.get("text", "")) > 0
    def name(self):
        return "MyFilter"

register_filter("MyFilter", MyFilter)
